reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal claim. The hypothesis `f : X → Y` with `UniformContinuousOn f A` is the standard Mathlib way to encode "f : A → Y is uniformly continuous" (by `uniformContinuousOn_iff_restrict` in Mathlib's Topology/UniformSpace/Basic.lean, these are equivalent). The conclusion provides: (i) existence of `g : closure A → Y`, (ii) `UniformContinuous g` (matching "g is uniformly continuous"), (iii) `g` agrees with `f` on `A` (matching the extension property — uses `subset_closure` to embed `A` into `closure A`), and (iv) uniqueness among all continuous functions extending `f` (matching "uniquely extended to a continuous function"). Quantifying uniqueness over continuous rather than uniformly continuous extensions matches the natural reading of the informal text and is mathematically correct since any two continuous maps agreeing on a dense set agree everywhere (Y metric ⇒ Hausdorff). All hypotheses (metric spaces, completeness of Y) match exactly.
